groups.explanation: Each word precedes BALL to form a compound word: FIREBALL, BASKETBALL, CANNONBALL, SNOWBALL.
Each word precedes STOP to form a common compound word or phrase: NONSTOP, FULL STOP, PIT STOP, BUS STOP.

groups.category: Add D to the End to Make a New Word
groups.explanation: Adding D to the end of each word makes a new common word: GRAND, BAND, WAND, FEND.

groups.category: Add E to the End to Make a New Word
groups.explanation: Adding E to the end of each word makes a new word: PLANE, SHINE, GRIME, TWINE.

groups.category: Add H to the Front to Make a New Word
groups.explanation: Adding H to the front of each word makes HOURS, HARDER, HEDGE, and HITCH.

groups.category: Add R to the End to Make a New Word
groups.explanation: Adding R to the end of each word spells CHAIR, OFFER, TOWER, and WATER.

groups.category: Add S to the Front to Make a New Word
groups.explanation: Adding S to the front of each word makes SLICK, STRAIN, SPACE, and SCARE.

groups.category: Add T to the End to Make a New Word
groups.explanation: Adding T to the end of each word spells HEART, EXACT, FOREST, and DIRECT.
Each word is a common name for a young animal: a baby horse, cow, sheep, or goat.
Sweet, sour, salty, and bitter are four of the recognized basic taste sensations.
Each word is part of a Grand Slam tournament venue: Wimbledon, Flushing Meadows (US Open), Roland Garros (French Open), and Melbourne Park (Australian Open).
These four months each have exactly 30 days.
Reef, tide, shelf, and shore are all distinct features or zones found in and around the ocean.
The four suits in a standard deck of cards.
Each word follows RAIN to form a common compound word or phrase: RAINBOW, RAINDROP, RAINCOAT, RAINFOREST.
Candle, lantern, torch, and lamp all produce light.
All four are actions naturally associated with birds.
Ranks found in a card deck.
You can carve a pumpkin, a turkey, wood, and ice — each is a natural object shaped by cutting.
All are common items typically folded after washing or use.
You can hatch an egg, a plan, a plot, or a scheme — all natural uses of the verb hatch.
Scarves, sweaters, mittens, and blankets are all classic items people make by knitting.
All four are flexible strands or strips that can literally be tied into a knot.
You can shuffle cards, feet, papers, and a playlist — all common uses of the word 'shuffle'.
All four are commonly squeezed to extract something from them.
You strike a match, strike a bell, strike a chord, and strike a deal — all pair naturally with the verb 'strike'.
All four are foods commonly toasted over heat or in a toaster.
All are common varieties of bread.
Each word follows APPLE to make a common food or drink compound: applesauce, apple juice, apple pie, apple cider.

groups.category: Add a letter to the front to make a new word: add W
groups.explanation: Placing W at the start of each word makes a new common word: WITCH, WHEEL, WRING, WEDGE.

groups.category: Add an H to make a new word
groups.explanation: Adding H at the front yields HAIR, HEAR, HOUR, HARM.

groups.category: Add G to the front to make a new word
groups.explanation: ROUND→GROUND, RAIN→GRAIN, RIND→GRIND, RIP→GRIP — adding G to the front of each creates a new common word.
Each word is also the name of a fencing weapon or blade type used in the sport of fencing.
Each word is a common English word that is also the name of a punctuation mark.

groups.category: Also a Type of Government Bond
groups.explanation: GILT, TREASURY, MUNICIPAL, and BEARER are all recognized categories of government-issued bonds.
Each name belongs to a recognized archangel in religious tradition.
Each word is an action a player takes during a card game: playing a trump, bluffing, folding, or dealing.

groups.category: Common words that are also knitting stitches
groups.explanation: CABLE, SEED, MOSS, and RIB are all everyday English words that are also named knitting stitch patterns.

groups.category: Common Words That Are Also Logical Operators
groups.explanation: AND, OR, NOT, and NOR are everyday English words that are also the core Boolean logical operators in computing and formal logic.
Each word reads the same backward.

groups.category: Remove the last letter to get a body part
groups.explanation: Drop the final letter to reveal ear, hip, lip, arm.
Navy, teal, cobalt, and azure are all recognized shades of blue.
All four are recognized measurements of time duration.
All can be scrambled: scrambled egg, scrambled signal, scrambled jets (launched fighters), scrambled message.
A clock face, coin face, cliff face, and playing-card face are all recognized named parts of these objects.
Each can be said to run.
Saturn has planetary rings, a tree trunk has growth rings, a bathtub leaves a ring, and boxing takes place in a ring.
Each has a kind of scale: animal, map, or musical.
You can draw a curtain (close it), draw a sword (unsheathe it), draw a bath (fill it), and draw a conclusion (reach one).
Major, minor, thesis, and seminar are all standard elements of university academic life.
Each can precede the word fly.

groups.category: Words that become a new word when reversed
groups.explanation: STEP→PETS, POOL→LOOP, STAR→RATS, MOOD→DOOM — each reverses to a different common English word.

groups.category: Words that become a new word when you add 'S' to the front
groups.explanation: Adding S to the front of each gives STRAIN, SLAUGHTER, SWORD, and SCRAM — all common words.

groups.category: Words that become new words when read backward
groups.explanation: Each reversed spells another valid word: DESSERTS, REWARD, SPOONS, WARTS.

groups.category: Words that rhyme with a number
groups.explanation: SUN rhymes with ONE, BLUE rhymes with TWO, HEAVEN rhymes with SEVEN, and DINE rhymes with NINE.

groups.category: Words Whose Letters Are All in the First Half of the Alphabet (A–M)
groups.explanation: Every letter in CLAIM, FLAME, MAGIC, and LLAMA falls within A through M, the first half of the alphabet.
Each word precedes MARKET to form a common compound: STOCK MARKET, FLEA MARKET, SUPERMARKET, BLACK MARKET.
COMPASS, CHART, BEARING, and PILOT are all everyday English words that are also standard navigational tools or terms used in sailing and aviation.
Each word is also the name of a Roman emperor: Nero, Titus, Hadrian, and Marcus (Aurelius).

groups.category: Also a Type of Chess Opening
groups.explanation: Each word is also the name of a well-known chess opening: the Sicilian, Dragon, English, and French defenses.
Each word is a common everyday word that is also a recognized subgenre of jazz music.
Parts or features of a camera.
These are defensive structures or barriers associated with castles.
Drink sizes used by a major coffee chain.

groups.category: Currencies that are also common words
groups.explanation: Each is a unit or nickname for currency and also an everyday English word.
All are throwing events in track and field.
Each is a recognisable part or feature of a bicycle.

groups.category: Remove the First Letter to Get a New Word
groups.explanation: Remove the first letter of each word to get a new common word: WHEAT→HEAT, KNAVE→NAVE, BRACE→RACE, STALE→TALE.
You cast a shadow, cast a net, cast a spell, and cast a vote.
A tent is pitched at a campsite, a ball is pitched in baseball, an idea is pitched to investors, and a voice can be pitched high or low.
Each can be wound up or wound around.
Each item is typically used or sold as a pair.
Each is well known for floating, whether on water or in the air.
Each of these has an arm as a feature.
Rocking cradle, rocking boat, rocking chair, and rocking horse all rock back and forth.
Each commonly has a 'bed': riverbed, truck bed, garden bed, nail bed.
Each has or is associated with a trunk: elephant trunk, car trunk, tree trunk, swimming trunks.
Each can have or use keys.
You can catch a cold, catch a bus, catch a wave, or catch a break.
You can whistle a tune, song, melody, or jingle — all musical things producible by whistling.
Each is a vessel used to travel across water.
Each word precedes 'house' to form a compound word: warehouse, powerhouse, full house, open house.

groups.category: Words That Are Also Fencing Moves... no — Words That Are Also Ballet Positions
groups.explanation: Each word is a standard named position or movement in classical ballet.

groups.category: Words that are also fencing terms
groups.explanation: PARRY, LUNGE, THRUST, and RIPOSTE are all common English words that are also specific moves in the sport of fencing.

groups.category: Words that are also military ranks
groups.explanation: Each is a common English word that is also a military rank.

groups.category: Words that are also tennis terms
groups.explanation: Each common word is also a term used in tennis.

groups.category: Words That Are Also Types of Malware
groups.explanation: Each word is a common English word that is also a recognized category of computer malware.
BLUEPRINT, BLUEBELL, BLUEBERRY, and BLUEBIRD are all common compound words formed by adding BLUE before each answer.
OVERFLOW, OVERNIGHT, OVERBOARD, OVERCOAT — all common compound words beginning with OVER.

groups.category: Words that sound like a letter of the alphabet
groups.explanation: AYE sounds like the letter I, JAY sounds like J, CUE sounds like Q, and TEE sounds like T.
TWEED, MUSLIN, DAMASK, and VELVET are all distinct woven or textile fabric types.
Each word is a common English word or phrase that is also the name of a specific knot used in sailing or climbing.

groups.category: Common Words That Are Also Keyboard Modifier Keys
groups.explanation: Each word is a common English word that is also a modifier key found on a standard keyboard.

groups.category: Common words that are also programming languages
groups.explanation: Each is a familiar English word that is also the name of a real programming language: Ruby, Swift, Rust, Go.

groups.category: Common words that are also statistical terms
groups.explanation: MEAN, RANGE, MODE, and SKEW are all everyday English words that are also core terms in descriptive statistics.
Each word sounds exactly like a letter of the alphabet: ARE=R, JAY=J, WHY=Y, KAY=K.
WON sounds like one, TOO sounds like two, FOR sounds like four, ATE sounds like eight.

groups.category: New word when S is added to the front
groups.explanation: Adding S to the front gives: STRAIN, SCREAM, SPRAY, SLAUGHTER.
GATE rhymes with eight, LINE rhymes with nine, HEAVEN rhymes with seven, and FLOOR rhymes with four.

groups.category: Spelled with only musical note letters (A–G)
groups.explanation: Every letter in each word is a musical note (A B C D E F G): BEAD, CAGE, FACE, DECADE.

Each can precede the word fish.

groups.category: Words spelled using only Roman numerals
groups.explanation: Each word is spelled using only the letters that serve as Roman numerals (I, V, X, L, C, D, M): LIVID, CIVIL, MIMIC, and VIVID use only L, I, V, C, M, D.

groups.category: Words using only the vowel E
groups.explanation: E is the only vowel appearing in each word.
